author: tersec
content:
`nimbus-ethrex-1` has ethrex claiming all of these are `INVALID` due to BAL issues:
- https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/block/0x0ae678dea9bab989e170ab4305042e80f14ed4eab5929246b9925fb253d0cad2
- https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/block/0x1d0e96bb82e4d9c84922577d6d88400ef97fb94d19ff5c32d54fdb06a8f66c64
- https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/block/0x7303a7a1376993cb1441bcf6e649a44e153399fc9e6063c6978fd98d41c1f0ad

which are all canonical and finalized

author: tersec
content: the most obvious observation here is that all 3 blocks were built by Erigon

author: tersec
content: hm, the payloads were orphaned, but I read that as valid-but-ignored-for some-reason <@808969530608451584> is that what Dora means here?

author: tersec
content: or can that actually be that they were Invalid?

author: pk910
content:
Dora does not validate any data.  It shows what it gets from clients.

Orphaned payload means the payload is not part of the canonical chain.
So dora received it and shows it,   but the next canonical block decided to build on an empty parent block  (likely because of the invalid BAL)

author: tersec
content: ok, so it probably is INVALID, checking with other nodes

author: povilas8490
content:
https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/block/0x1d0e96bb82e4d9c84922577d6d88400ef97fb94d19ff5c32d54fdb06a8f66c64

`nethermind` in `grandine-nethermind-1` has flagged this as INVALID too.

Error: `InvalidBlockLevelAccessList: Suggested block-level access list contained incorrect changes for 0xef998b3b26fb38c38707334a733896f3ab63ad4a at index 83.`

author: tersec
content: so, I apologize, I misinterpreted Dora

author: tersec
content: these are genuinely invalid EL blocks

author: tersec
content: I checked our `nimbus-nethermind-1` node and yeah, see the same invalids from Nethermind
